# grackle

> Static scanner that detects fork-triggerable CI coding agents with repository write access in GitHub Actions and GitLab CI workflows. When an AI coding agent runs on untrusted fork input in a job that can write to the repo and nothing checks who triggered it, prompt injection becomes remote code execution and repository takeover under the CI token. grackle finds that exact composition statically, before it merges. Apache-2.0 licensed and free for commercial use.

grackle is a single Rust binary. It parses GitHub Actions and GitLab CI workflows, proves fork reachability, author and merge gates, and repository write capability, then reports only the compositions that are actually exploitable. Findings carry a severity, a confidence, control-framework references, the offending workflow block, and a dynamic secure-fix write-up. It outputs SARIF, GitLab SAST, CycloneDX SBOM, JUnit, JSON, HTML, Markdown, YAML, CSV, and XML. Releases ship with SLSA Build Level 3 provenance and CycloneDX SBOMs, and release artifacts are Sigstore-signed.

## Install

- [Installation](https://cpeoples.github.io/grackle/#install): download a prebuilt binary from releases, or build from source with the Rust toolchain
- [Prebuilt binaries](https://github.com/cpeoples/grackle/releases): macOS, Linux, and Windows archives attached to each release
- [Snap Store](https://snapcraft.io/grackle): snap install grackle
- [Build from source](https://github.com/cpeoples/grackle): cargo install --git https://github.com/cpeoples/grackle.git
- [Source code](https://github.com/cpeoples/grackle): GitHub repository (Apache-2.0)
- [Documentation](https://cpeoples.github.io/grackle/): full docs site

## Quick start

- [CLI reference](https://cpeoples.github.io/grackle/cli/): flags, formats, and self-test
- [Detection model](https://cpeoples.github.io/grackle/detection-model/): how fork reachability, gates, and write capability are proven
- [CI/CD integration](https://cpeoples.github.io/grackle/ci-cd/): GitHub Actions and GitLab CI, plus pull request and merge request comments
- [Output formats](https://cpeoples.github.io/grackle/output-formats/): every format, confidence banding, and dynamic remediation
- [Remediations](https://cpeoples.github.io/grackle/remediations/): how the corrected workflow is generated
- [Limitations](https://cpeoples.github.io/grackle/limitations/): what this layer of defense does and does not cover

## Distribution and supply-chain

- License: Apache-2.0
- Language: Rust (edition 2021)
- Provenance: [SLSA Build Level 3](https://slsa.dev/spec/v1.0/levels#build-l3)
- SBOM: CycloneDX, attached to every GitHub release
- Signing: Sigstore-verified release artifacts

## Output formats

- SARIF 2.1.0 (GitHub code scanning)
- GitLab SAST JSON (GitLab Security Dashboard)
- CycloneDX 1.5 SBOM
- JUnit XML
- JSON, HTML, Markdown, YAML, CSV, XML

## Project links

- [Issues](https://github.com/cpeoples/grackle/issues)
- [Releases and changelog](https://github.com/cpeoples/grackle/releases)
- [Author](https://github.com/cpeoples)
